title: Parathesis macrophylla
taxonomy: Parathesis macrophylla is classified within the family Clusiaceae, commonly known as the St. John's Wort family. This family consists of flowering plants that are primarily found in tropical and subtropical regions. Parathesis macrophylla is a species within the genus Parathesis, which is native to Central and South America. The classification of Parathesis macrophylla is further supported by its characteristics, such as its large leaves and woody habit. The exact classification of Parathesis macrophylla may vary depending on the source and the current understanding of plant taxonomy.
uses: Parathesis macrophylla is known for its various medicinal uses. The plant has been traditionally used to treat fever and respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. Its extracts have been studied for their potential antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. Additionally, Parathesis macrophylla is also used in folk medicine to treat digestive issues and as an anti-diarrheal agent. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy of the plant for these uses.
benefits: Parathesis macrophylla is a plant species that has been traditionally used in medicine, and research has identified some potential health benefits associated with it. constituents: Parathesis macrophylla contains active constituents such as flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids. parts: Parathesis macrophylla is a plant species that has been used in traditional medicine, particularly in the Amazon region.
